steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content:
To the left of the blank: “Popper's theory” — a noun phrase serving as the subject.

To the right: “that scientific hypotheses must be refutable” (a noun clause), “termed the criterion of falsifiability” (a participial phrase), and then “rejects” governing a direct object.

steps.title: Apply the structures to the choices
steps.content:
'Dictating' (present participle) creates a participial phrase modifying the subject, allowing 'rejects' to be the sole main verb.

The other options are main verbs ('was dictating,' 'dictates') that create a double-verb problem, or don't function as a participle ('dictated').

That matches choice C — C is correct.
